title: Untrusted Markup, Formula Injection, Paths, Logs, and Plugin Boundaries · TypeScript Build Room
summary: A disability-led public-interest technology team incrementally builds safe untrusted-content processing boundary from original deterministic HTTP, robots, sitemap, HTML, browser, and report fixtures while retaining prior TypeScript, Node, web, Git, accessibility, security, testing, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of safe untrusted-content processing boundary while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit…
objectives: Render findings through text-safe sinks or context-specific encoding and narrowly reviewed sanitization.
Preserve data while neutralizing spreadsheet formula triggers under an explicit export and consumer contract.
Generate owned filenames, confine paths, reject traversal and unsafe archive entries, and handle collisions and Unicode deliberately.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Safe Rendering Sinks
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop TypeScript team handles case 6416 during an origin-fairness and event-loop capacity rehearsal. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to defend HTML, CSV, paths, archives, logs, terminals, and plugins against untrusted crawler content; every external value remains unknown until runtime admission produces an owned value; then change one HTML repair, Cheerio mode, selector, unknown value, record schema, link relation, or metadata case and retain rejected evidence. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Safe Rendering Sinks.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for render findings through text-safe sinks or context-specific encoding and narrowly reviewed sanitization. For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Safe Rendering Sinks, keep Safe Rendering Sinks connected to executable TypeScript authorization, URL, runtime admission, Fetch, Promise, browser, accessibility, report, package, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Safe Rendering Sinks has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Plan the evidence sequence · Workshop Safe Rendering Sinks, name the owner, authorization, source and emitted revision, Node, TypeScript, URL, robots, unknown value, Fetch, Promise, AbortSignal, browser, accessibility, report, handle, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Safe Rendering Sinks precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Plan the evidence sequence · Workshop Safe Rendering Sinks, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A workshop TypeScript team handles case 2223 during an isolated browser, package, and restore smoke. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to defend HTML, CSV, paths, archives, logs, terminals, and plugins against untrusted crawler content; every Promise, stream, listener, timer, socket, file, browser context, and report sink needs one cleanup owner; then change one test, mutation, package, browser binary, container, capacity, incident, restore, rollback, or residual-risk condition and reconcile release evidence. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Render findings through text-safe sinks or context-specific encoding and narrowly reviewed sanitization.

For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Plan the evidence sequence · Workshop Safe Rendering Sinks, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Escaping HTML once makes content safe in every output context.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Plugin Capability
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-TypeScript function headed "// Evidence: crawler-ts-plugin-capability-f21vwc" that uses case 3572 to defend HTML, CSV, paths, archives, logs, terminals, and plugins against untrusted crawler content. Keep defaults runnable and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import Node APIs, open streams or sockets, resolve DNS, parse through Cheerio, launch Playwright or axe-core, access files or packages, retain credentials, leave handles, or claim native runtime, accessibility, privacy, legal, load, container, restore, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Node 24.18.0, TypeScript 7.0.2 and the TypeScript 6.0.2 compatibility compiler API, Cheerio 1.2.0, robots-parser 3.0.1, p-limit 7.3.0, csv-stringify 6.8.1, Playwright 1.61.1, axe-core 4.12.1, original fixtures, loopback servers, emitted-runtime and handle tests, controlled DNS and browser environments, accessibility review, packages, containers, capacity, backup, restore, rollback, legal review, and release gates.

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Plugin Capability.
